properties.name: driftguard.enabled
properties.description: Enables DriftGuard auto-configuration.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors
properties.description: Detector definitions registered in the detection engine.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors-enabled
properties.description: Enables detector definitions declared through application.yml.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].enabled
properties.description: Enables or disables one detector definition without removing it from configuration.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].name
properties.description: Unique detector name used in DriftEvent.detector and runtime state keys.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].algorithm
properties.description: Detector algorithm: page-hinkley, adwin, psi, ks or chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].services
properties.description: Filter by MetricKey.service. An empty list matches any service.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].metrics
properties.description: Filter by MetricKey.metric. An empty list matches any metric.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].operations
properties.description: Filter by MetricKey.operation. An empty list matches any operation.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].instances
properties.description: Filter by MetricKey.instance. An empty list matches any instance id.

properties.name: driftguard.metrics.enabled
properties.description: Enables the Micrometer listener for detection pipeline runtime metrics when a MeterRegistry is present.

properties.name: driftguard.metrics.kafka-enabled
properties.description: Enables Kafka-specific Micrometer metrics for the detection topology when a MeterRegistry is present.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].baseline-window-size
properties.description: Baseline window size for PSI, KS and chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].current-window-size
properties.description: Current window size for PSI, KS and chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].window-size
properties.description: Adaptive window size for ADWIN.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].min-sub-window-size
properties.description: Minimum size of each window side when ADWIN searches for a cut.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].warmup-samples
properties.description: Number of initial samples used by Page-Hinkley to warm up the baseline.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].buckets
properties.description: Bucket count for PSI and chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].warning-threshold
properties.description: Warning threshold for PSI or Page-Hinkley depending on the selected algorithm.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].critical-threshold
properties.description: Critical threshold for PSI or Page-Hinkley depending on the selected algorithm.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].warning-p-value
properties.description: P-value boundary for warning events in KS and chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].critical-p-value
properties.description: P-value boundary for critical events in KS and chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].delta
properties.description: Sensitivity/confidence parameter for ADWIN and Page-Hinkley.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].alpha
properties.description: Mean adaptation speed in Page-Hinkley.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].direction
properties.description: Mean-shift direction for Page-Hinkley: UP for growth, DOWN for drops.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].epsilon
properties.description: Small PSI smoothing value that prevents division by zero.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].critical-multiplier
properties.description: Score multiplier after which ADWIN marks an event as critical.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].min-expected-count
properties.description: Minimum expected bucket frequency for chi-square.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].emission-policy.min-consecutive-signals
properties.description: Number of consecutive algorithm signals required before publishing a DriftEvent.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].emission-policy.cooldown
properties.description: Minimum pause between DriftEvent publications for one detector and metric stream.

properties.name: driftguard.detectors[].emission-policy.recovery-consecutive-normal
properties.description: Number of consecutive normal observations that close a drift episode and allow a new event.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.enabled
properties.description: Enables the DriftGuard Kafka Streams topology.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.bootstrap-servers
properties.description: Kafka bootstrap servers for the DriftGuard topology.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.application-id
properties.description: Kafka Streams application id.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.input-topics
properties.description: Topics containing JSON MetricPoint messages.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.output-topic
properties.description: Topic where the topology writes JSON DriftEvent messages.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.state-dir
properties.description: Local directory for Kafka Streams state stores.

properties.name: driftguard.kafka.auto-startup
properties.description: Whether Kafka Streams topology starts automatically with the Spring context.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.enabled
properties.description: Whether DriftGuard maps emitted drift events to user-facing alerts.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.logging-enabled
properties.description: Whether the starter creates the default SLF4J alert sink. It can run alongside custom sinks.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.webhook.enabled
properties.description: Whether the starter creates a generic HTTP webhook alert sink.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.webhook.url
properties.description: HTTP endpoint that receives DriftAlert JSON payloads.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.webhook.timeout
properties.description: HTTP webhook request timeout.

properties.name: driftguard.alerts.webhook.headers
properties.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with webhook alert requests.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.enabled
properties.description: Whether the Micrometer input adapter polls selected meters and publishes them as MetricPoint observations.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.auto-startup
properties.description: Whether the Micrometer input poller starts with the Spring context.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.poll-interval
properties.description: Pause between Micrometer input samples.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters
properties.description: Micrometer meters sampled by the input adapter.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].name
properties.description: Micrometer meter name.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].type
properties.description: How the adapter reads the meter: gauge, counter, timer-mean or timer-max.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].service
properties.description: MetricKey.service value for the published MetricPoint.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].metric
properties.description: MetricKey.metric value for the published MetricPoint.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].operation
properties.description: Optional MetricKey.operation value for the published MetricPoint.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].instance
properties.description: Optional MetricKey.instance value for the published MetricPoint.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].kind
properties.description: Optional MetricPoint kind override.

properties.name: driftguard.micrometer-input.meters[].tags
properties.description: Micrometer tags used to find a concrete meter and copied to MetricPoint tags.
